overview: My teaching centers AI literacy, critical platform thinking, beginner-friendly technical instruction, and project-based learning. I design courses and assignments that help students understand how computational systems work, evaluate their social implications, and build usable prototypes.
philosophy: I aim to make technical and theoretical ideas accessible without oversimplifying them. Students should leave my courses able to reason about how platforms work, interrogate their social consequences, and apply that thinking in hands-on projects.

courses.title: Current State and Future of Generative AI
courses.description: I designed and developed this core course for Notre Dame's ESTEEM master's program in Engineering, Science and Technology Entrepreneurship, which combines technical and business education with a year-long technology-commercialization capstone. The course examines how generative AI systems work, their current capabilities and limitations, practical tools for entrepreneurial and product contexts, emerging trends, and the ethical and legal questions that accompany their use.

courses.title: Elements of Computing I
courses.description: An introduction to the technical and social dimensions of computing, emphasizing computational thinking, problem-solving, and programming literacy using Python.

courses.title: Human Computer Interaction
courses.description: An in-depth introduction to HCI history, principles, design methods, evaluation techniques, visual design, and emerging areas of interactive technology.

courses.title: Database Concepts
courses.description: A course on relational database design and implementation, including entity-relationship modeling, querying, normalization, integrity constraints, and database-backed systems.
